sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, ip_address INET, is_active BOOLEAN);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, ip_address, is_active) VALUES (1, '192.168.1.5', true);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, ip_address, is_active) VALUES (2, '192.168.1.10', false);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, ip_address, is_active) VALUES (3, NULL, NULL);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, ip_address, is_active) VALUES (4, '192.168.2.1', true);
SELECT id, ip_address, 'and'(is_active, is_active = true) AS combined_active FROM test_table WHERE 'and'(is_active, ip_address >> inet '192.168.1.0/24') = true ORDER BY combined_active DESC;
SELECT id, 'and'(is_active, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE is_active IS NULL) = 0) AS final_active FROM test_table ORDER BY final_active;
SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE 'and'(is_active, ip_address >> inet '192.168.1.5') IS TRUE;
SELECT COUNT(id) FROM test_table GROUP BY 'and'(is_active, ip_address >> inet '192.168.1.0/24') HAVING COUNT(id) > 1;
WITH recursive temp AS (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE ip_address IS NOT NULL) SELECT 'and'(is_active, id IN (SELECT id FROM temp)) AS result FROM test_table;
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, user_status BOOLEAN DEFAULT NULL);
INSERT INTO another_table (id, user_status) VALUES (1, true), (2, false), (3, NULL);
SELECT t1.id, 'and'(t1.is_active, t2.user_status) AS overall_status FROM test_table t1 JOIN another_table t2 ON t1.id = t2.id WHERE t1.ip_address >> inet '192.168.1.0/24';
DELETE FROM test_table WHERE 'and'(is_active, ip_address >> inet '192.168.1.5') IS FALSE;
SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE 'and'(ip_address IS NOT NULL, ip_address >> inet '192.168.1.0/24') ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 5;
